hero.heading: Build It. Ship It.
hero.description: From a single AI assistant to a full autonomous company — CapiBot gives you 43+ built-in tools, 600+ skills, 280+ agents, and complete control over how much autonomy your agents get. Private. Secure. Yours.

Full data privacy — nothing leaves your machine

faq.heading: Common questions
faq.subheading: Everything you need to know about CapiBot.

items.a: CapiBot is a private AI agent orchestration platform. Every user gets their own dedicated instance with 280+ specialized AI agents that can collaborate, execute tasks, and form autonomous AI companies — all running on infrastructure that's exclusively yours.

All plans include every feature, every AI model, and unlimited agents and companies. The only difference between plans is the number of monthly credits. Standard models cost 1 credit per action, while premium models like Sonnet (5 credits) and Opus (10 credits) cost more. You choose how to spend your credits.

items.q: What AI models can I use?
items.a: All of them — on every plan. CapiBot supports Claude (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus), GPT-4o, Google Gemini, Kimi, Qwen, GLM, MiniMax, and local models via Ollama. You can configure priority order and automatic failover between providers.

AI Companies are CapiBot's flagship feature. A CEO agent drafts a business plan, hires specialist agents from a roster of 280+, creates tasks, and executes — with configurable human oversight at every step.

In managed mode, all CEO-created tasks require human approval before execution. You can approve plans, hiring decisions, and budgets through the Mission Control dashboard. Or switch to fully autonomous mode when you're confident.
Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack, and a built-in web chat in Mission Control. Each channel supports rich messages, media, and voice transcription where applicable.
Completely. Every user gets their own dedicated instance with its own database. Your data is never shared with other users. There's no telemetry, no data collection, and no shared infrastructure.

Credits are consumed when your agents take actions that require an AI model — sending messages, processing tasks, making decisions. System operations like heartbeat checks and scheduled monitoring are free and don't use credits.

items.q: How do I connect external apps?
items.a: Through Composio, which provides access to thousands of apps via a unified API including Gmail, Google Docs, Notion, and more. You can also write custom skills as Markdown files — no code changes needed.
Pick a plan, complete checkout, choose your username, and your private instance is live within 60 seconds at username.capibot.io. No setup, no configuration, no ops work required.

openClaw.heading: CapiBot vs OpenClaw
openClaw.description: Both are open-source AI agent platforms. But CapiBot was built to solve the problems OpenClaw users keep running into: security vulnerabilities, runaway costs, no team management, and no real human oversight.

shortVersion.title: Security First
shortVersion.desc: CapiBot blocks 80+ dangerous shell patterns by default. OpenClaw runs arbitrary code with no guardrails.

shortVersion.title: Team Management
shortVersion.desc: CapiBot has 280+ agents organized in a hierarchy with roles, tiers, and categories across 18 specializations. OpenClaw has a flat agent model.

shortVersion.title: Human Oversight
shortVersion.desc: CapiBot's managed mode requires approval for plans, hiring, and budgets. OpenClaw has no built-in approval workflow.

openClaw.whySubtitle: Six areas where CapiBot goes beyond what OpenClaw offers.

advantages.title: Security & Sandboxing
advantages.description: 80+ blocked shell patterns, API key guards, audit trails, and HMAC webhook verification out of the box.

advantages.title: Agent Hierarchy
advantages.description: CEOs, specialists, and interns across 12+ categories — not a flat list of interchangeable bots.

advantages.title: AI Companies
advantages.description: Full company lifecycle: briefing, planning, approval, and operating — with human oversight at every gate.

advantages.title: Mission Control
advantages.description: A 28-panel professional dashboard vs. a basic chat interface. Real observability and management.

advantages.title: Multi-Provider Failover
advantages.description: Automatic failover across 6 LLM providers with configurable priority and per-agent model overrides.

advantages.title: Memory & Knowledge
advantages.description: Persistent semantic memory with pgvector, SOUL.md personalities, and shared knowledge bases.

openClaw.comparisonSubtitle: A detailed look at what each platform offers.
forYouItems: You want AI agents that actually work as a team, not isolated chatbots
Security matters — you need sandboxing, audit trails, and access control
You want human oversight without micromanaging every action
You need a real dashboard, not just a chat window
You want to self-host and own your data completely
You need multi-provider LLM support with automatic failover
You want an open-source core with no vendor lock-in
